# Test fixtures — the synthetic-first policy

This directory is the single home for **committed** test fixtures. The policy
resolves a tension that is otherwise decided ad hoc, test by test: byte-level
validation wants real game data, and the repository must never contain any.

## The two modes

**1. Committed synthetic fixtures (this directory) — run everywhere.**
Every file under `tests/fixtures/` is synthetic: produced by our own encoders,
or hand-assembled byte by byte from the format specs in
[docs/fa/formats/](../../docs/fa/formats/). They are covered by the normal
test suites on every preset leg, locally and in CI. CI never sees a byte of
game data.

**2. Real-install validation (`FX_FA_ROOT`) — local benches only.**
Tests that need a licensed FA install register only when `FX_FA_ROOT` points
at one (CMake cache variable or environment variable). They carry the ctest
label `integration`, are skipped in CI by construction (CI has no install),
and run on the Fedora and Windows benches. What gets *committed* from these
runs is hashes and other facts about the data — e.g.
[`tests/integration/fa-extract.sha256`](../integration/fa-extract.sha256) —
never the data itself.

A format's proof therefore has two layers: the synthetic fixtures pin the
codec's behavior everywhere, and the `integration` label re-verifies it
against the real install on the benches. New test work cites this file and
slots into one of the two modes; a test that "needs a real sample in git" is
mis-designed — either synthesize the sample or move the check behind
`FX_FA_ROOT`.

## Rules for committed fixtures

- **Synthetic only.** Generated by an `fx_lib` encoder, or hand-crafted. A
  file extracted from the game — even a tiny or texty one — is game content
  and stays out (that includes anything produced by `fx unpack`).
- **Provenance is documented.** Every fixture states, in the per-directory
  README section below (or a comment in the test that generates it), what it
  exercises and exactly how it was produced, so it can be regenerated from
  scratch.
- **Small.** Fixtures are bytes to a few KB — the smallest input that
  exercises the path. Anything larger should be generated at test time
  instead of committed.
- **Never an FA asset extension.** The gitignore bans uppercase FA
  extensions (`*.LIB`, `*.PIC`, `*.SH`, `*.M`, …) precisely so real assets
  cannot slip in; fixtures use lowercase names with `.bin` or a lowercase
  format tag (e.g. `.dcl`) so that firewall never needs a carve-out. Fuzz
  seeds follow the same rule as `fuzz/corpus/<harness>/seed-*.bin` — see
  [docs/development.md § Fuzzing](../../docs/development.md#fuzzing).

## Loading fixtures in tests

`fx_tests` gets `FX_FIXTURES_DIR` (this directory, absolute) as a compile
definition from `tests/CMakeLists.txt`; load fixtures through the helper:

```cpp
#include "support/fixture.h"
auto bytes = fx_test::load_fixture("blast/adler-ai.dcl");
```

## Fixture inventory

### blast/

- `adler-ai.dcl` — Mark Adler's `blast.c` reference PKWARE-DCL stream
  (`00 04 82 24 25 8F 80 7F`): litmode 0, dictbits 4, decompresses to
  `"AIAIAIAIAIAIA"` (13 bytes). Hand-transcribed from the zlib `contrib/blast`
  documentation; the canonical known-answer vector for the DCL decompressor.
